_comments: TODO gear: items required or usable for this reaction
TODO traits: character traits that modify this reaction
REVIEW modifiers: list of {range:[min,max], description} skill modifiers

Acrobatics.description: In combat, the character leaps or rolls dramatically to avoid an incoming attack.
Acrobatics.success: The attack misses entirely; the character ends up in a better position.
Acrobatics.failure: The character's dodge fails and they are hit normally.
Acrobatics.phenomenal: The dodge moves the character behind the attacker, granting a free attack.
Acrobatics.catastrophic: The character stumbles into the blow, taking damage with no defence, and falls prone.

modifiers.description: Carrying more than Light encumbrance
Slippery or uneven surface
Surprised (no warning the attack was coming)

Acting.description: The character collapses and convincingly mimics unconsciousness or death during or after a fight.
Acting.success: Enemies or witnesses believe the character is incapacitated and leave them or lower their guard.
Acting.failure: An enemy notices movement or breathing and knows the ruse.
Acting.phenomenal: Even a close inspection fails to reveal the deception; the character may act freely on a later turn.
Acting.catastrophic: The character is so unconvincing that observers immediately attack to make sure.

modifiers.description: Opponent has reason to confirm the kill
Opponent is suspicious or was not fooled recently
Character is already wounded and bleeding

Body Language.description: The character observes a person's posture, facial expressions, and gestures to gauge their emotional state.
Body Language.success: The character accurately reads whether the person is calm, nervous, angry, or lying.
Body Language.failure: The character misreads the person's state or gets no useful information.
Body Language.phenomenal: The character reads extremely subtle cues, gaining nuanced understanding of the person's feelings and intentions.
Body Language.catastrophic: The character completely misreads the person, perhaps confusing fear for aggression or deception for sincerity.

modifiers.description: Subject is of a very different culture
Viewing from distance or in poor light
Extended observation of more than a minute

Body Language.description: The character observes a person's posture, facial expressions, and gestures to gauge their emotional state.
Body Language.success: The character accurately reads whether the person is calm, nervous, angry, or lying.
Body Language.failure: The character misreads the person's state or gets no useful information.
Body Language.phenomenal: The character reads extremely subtle cues, gaining nuanced understanding of the person's feelings and intentions.
Body Language.catastrophic: The character completely misreads the person, perhaps confusing fear for aggression or deception for sincerity.

modifiers.description: Subject is of a very different culture
Subject is deliberately controlling their expression
Extended observation (1+ minute)

actions: Conceal a Weapon on One's Person
Body Language.description: By watching how a person moves and holds their body, the character infers whether they are concealing a weapon.
Body Language.success: The character correctly identifies that the person is armed and roughly where the weapon is.
Body Language.phenomenal: The character pinpoints the exact weapon and notes the person's readiness to use it.
Body Language.catastrophic: The character incorrectly concludes the person is armed (or unarmed), potentially causing an incident.

modifiers.description: Subject is wearing a bulky cloak or robe
Character is actively suspicious of the subject

Brawling.description: The character uses trained unarmed technique to deflect or redirect an incoming strike.
Brawling.success: The attack is deflected; no damage is taken.
Brawling.failure: The parry fails; the attack connects and damage is dealt normally.
Brawling.phenomenal: The parry is so clean it leaves the attacker momentarily off-balance; the character may counter-attack freely.
Brawling.catastrophic: The parry collapses; the character takes full damage and their weapon may be knocked aside or broken.

modifiers.description: Parrying a two-handed weapon with a one-handed weapon
Parrying a flail (chain catches the blocking weapon)
Using a weapon or hand that has a parry bonus noted
Off-hand parry (varies by training level)
Parrying after already parrying once this turn
Using Kick (parry modifier +0.5 vs base)

Brawling.description: In combat, the character sideseps to avoid an incoming attack.
Brawling.failure: The character's dodge fails and they are hit normally.
Brawling.phenomenal: The dodge moves the character behind the attacker, granting a free attack.
Brawling.catastrophic: The character stumbles into the blow, taking damage with no defence, and falls prone.

modifiers.description: Flanked or attacked from behind (-2 to all active defences)
Already used a dodge this turn
Stunned or injured

Concentration.description: The character uses disciplined mental techniques to endure questioning without revealing sensitive information.
Concentration.success: The character gives nothing away during this session; the interrogator gains no new information.
Concentration.failure: The character's concentration slips; minor useful information may be extracted.
Concentration.phenomenal: The character remains perfectly composed, giving nothing away and possibly misleading the interrogator.
Concentration.catastrophic: The character's mental defences collapse; they reveal more than intended.

modifiers.description: Character is sleep-deprived or starved
Physical torture accompanies the interrogation
Character has prepared mental mantras in advance

Concentration.description: Through controlled breathing and mental focus, the character suppresses visible signs of pain.
Concentration.success: The character appears unaffected by pain; they can act normally for the current scene.
Concentration.phenomenal: The character is so composed under pain that observers may question whether they were actually struck.
Concentration.catastrophic: The mental effort backfires; the character is actually more impaired by the pain.

modifiers.description: Injury is catastrophic (more than half HP lost)
Character has been trained to endure pain

Detect Lies.description: The character closely watches a speaker for inconsistencies in tone, eye movement, or phrasing that indicate deception.
Detect Lies.success: The character correctly determines whether the person is lying.
Detect Lies.failure: The character gains no reliable reading.
Detect Lies.phenomenal: Not only does the character detect the lie, they also intuit approximately what the truth is.
Detect Lies.catastrophic: The character mistakes truth for a lie or a lie for truth, drawing a completely false conclusion.

modifiers.description: Acting-3 penalty applies: skilled liars are harder to catch
Subject is of IQ higher than the observer
Observer knows the subject personally
Extended questioning session (10+ minutes)

Detect Lies.description: The character listens to a witness's account and evaluates whether they are being truthful and complete.
Detect Lies.success: The character correctly judges the witness as truthful, evasive, or lying.
Detect Lies.failure: The character cannot reliably assess the witness.
Detect Lies.phenomenal: The character identifies exactly which parts of the account are false and which are being withheld.
Detect Lies.catastrophic: The character dismisses a truthful witness or places full trust in a liar.

modifiers.description: Witness is calm and experienced at deception
Observer has prior knowledge of the events described

Escape.description: The character works their bound wrists or ankles to loosen and slip through rope restraints.
Escape.success: The character escapes the bonds and is free.
Escape.failure: The bonds hold; no progress made.
Escape.phenomenal: The character escapes so quickly that no one notices until they are long free.
Escape.catastrophic: The attempt tightens the bonds; the character takes damage to their wrists and is more firmly restrained.

modifiers.description: Hands bound behind back
Knot-Tying roll succeeded by 5+
Wet rope
Character prepared by flexing muscles during binding

Escape.description: The character uses flexibility, improvised tools, or environmental features to get out of a secured space.
Escape.success: The character finds a means of egress and escapes.
Escape.failure: The room is too secure; no exit is found.
Escape.phenomenal: The character escapes and leaves the room looking undisturbed, concealing their escape.
Escape.catastrophic: The escape attempt alerts guards or damages evidence the character needed.

modifiers.description: Room is specifically designed as a cell
Hands are also bound
Character has an improvised tool

Weapon (Axes-Hammers-Picks).description: The character uses the haft or head of their hatchet to block an incoming blow.
Weapon (Axes-Hammers-Picks).success: The attack is deflected; no damage is taken.
Weapon (Axes-Hammers-Picks).failure: The parry fails; the attack connects and damage is dealt normally.
Weapon (Axes-Hammers-Picks).phenomenal: The parry is so clean it leaves the attacker momentarily off-balance; the character may counter-attack freely.
Weapon (Axes-Hammers-Picks).catastrophic: The parry collapses; the character takes full damage and their weapon may be knocked aside or broken.

Weapon (Curved Swords).catastrophic: The parry collapses; the character takes full damage and their weapon may be knocked aside or broken.
